passage.title: Center Choice Cards
passage.text: Our group keeps a helper chart beside the rug. During the first break, Mina looks at it before seat work. The helper chart has choice cards for blocks, art, and puzzles for the day's job. Our teacher asks us to pick the right basket and sit down. Sometimes we compare today's choice card with yesterday's choice card and notice one small change. We like the helper chart because it helps us share the room fairly.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the helper chart is used.
The first sentence says the helper chart is beside the rug.

questions.prompt: When does Mina use the helper chart?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Mina uses it during the first break.

questions.prompt: What does the helper chart have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the helper chart has choice cards.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
